title: Lepanthopsis dentifera
intro: Lepanthopsis dentifera, also known as Dentifera orchid, is a species of flowering orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepanthopsis dentifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lepanthopsis dentifera is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in tinctures and essential oil preparations to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Lepanthopsis dentifera is a species of orchid in the genus Lepanthopsis. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae. Lepanthopsis dentifera is a small, terrestrial orchid native to Central America. The species is classified within the tribe Cranichideae, which consists of small, leafy orchids. Lepanthopsis dentifera is further classified into the subtribe Spiranthinae.
